// backend/src/services/weatherService.ts
import axios from "axios";
import fs from "fs";
import path from "path";
import type { WeatherData } from "../types.js";
import { logInfo, logError } from "../logs.js";

const MOCK_FILE = path.join(process.cwd(), "backend/data/weather.mock.json");

/**
 * Core function that fetches weather (from OpenWeatherMap or mock)
 */
export async function fetchWeather(lat?: number, lon?: number, city?: string): Promise<WeatherData[]> {
  try {
    let data: WeatherData[] = [];

    if (process.env.OPENWEATHERMAP_API_KEY) {
      let url = "";
      if (lat !== undefined && lon !== undefined) {
        url = `https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?lat=${lat}&lon=${lon}&appid=${process.env.OPENWEATHERMAP_API_KEY}&units=metric`;
      } else if (city) {
        url = `https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q=${city}&appid=${process.env.OPENWEATHERMAP_API_KEY}&units=metric`;
      }

      if (url) {
        const resp = await axios.get(url, { timeout: 10000 });
        data = [resp.data].map(d => ({ ...d, locationLabel: { en: d.name } }));
        logInfo("[weatherService] Weather fetched from OpenWeatherMap", { count: data.length });
        return data;
      }
    }

    // fallback to mock
    const mockData = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(MOCK_FILE, "utf-8"));
    data = mockData.map((d: any) => ({ ...d, locationLabel: { en: d.location } }));
    logInfo("[weatherService] Weather loaded from mock", { count: data.length });
    return data;
  } catch (err: any) {
    logError("[weatherService] Failed to fetch weather, using mock", { error: err.message });
    const mockData = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(MOCK_FILE, "utf-8"));
    return mockData.map((d: any) => ({ ...d, locationLabel: { en: d.location } }));
  }
}

/**
 * Export aliases so router can use them
 */
export const getWeather = fetchWeather;

// Dummy refresh function (just calls fetchWeather for now)
export async function refreshWeatherCache(lat?: number, lon?: number, city?: string): Promise<WeatherData[]> {
  // You could implement cache invalidation here if you have caching
  return fetchWeather(lat, lon, city);
}
